title: MARLA'S MALL - 211info
text: MARLA'S MALL MARLA'S MALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift store with books, toys, household items, new hygiene products, knick-knacks, and clothing including interview or work-appropriate attire. Offers volunteer work experience and credit recovery for students working at the store. Also accepts donations of new and gently used items. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Thrift Store: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 8am-11am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: June 16th-July 17th Monday-Thursday 8:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 524-2309 Main phone Main phone (541) 524-2309 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Thrift store: Walk in Donations/Volunteering: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Baker County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Entry at the corner of 9th Street and G Street. Brick building in the parking lot between Baker High School and the football stadium. Services: Baby Clothing Bedding/Linen Bedding/Linen Donation Programs Children's Clothing Clothing Donation Programs Kitchenware Kitchenware Donation Programs Maternity Clothing Personal/Grooming Supplies Small Kitchen Appliances Thrift Shops Work Clothing Youth Community Service Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: MATERIAL GOODS - BULLDOG BUBBLES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
